channel.title: Comments for page "Top Rated Pages in the Last 30 Days"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Top Rated Pages in the Last 30 Days"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
HELP THIS TALK PAGE IS BEING COMPLETELY AND UTTERLY IGNORED!

Best way to get a staff member's attention for something is to send them a wikidot PM, rather than repeatedly editing a single post in a thread that people don't check that often.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It works for me. Maybe it's your browser?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is it just me or does the pagination thingy at the bottom of the "Top Rated SCPs This Month" block not work? The page just reloads with a new URL, but nothing changes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Remember when I complained stated that March was missing from the Top Rated Pages By Month 2018 page? Well, now June needs to be added too.

Edit: Where is the response? I am 100% certain I’m not doing it myself. A staff member needs to do it.

Another edit…: WHERE IS THE RESPONSE AND PAGE EDIT? IS IT BECAUSE JUNE 2018 WAS PRIDE MONTH, DISGRACING THE WIKI?

HELP THIS TALK PAGE IS BEING COMPLETELY AND UTTERLY IGNORED!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Untagged SCPs appear in that section, as that section is basically a catch-all for any page that isn't tagged SCP, Tale, or GOI-Format. Once it's been tagged, it'll move to the first section.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That block contains articles that aren't GoI's, Tales, or SCPs.

If an SCP is in that block, it means it's improperly tagged.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Question: Maybe I'm missing something, but what's the difference between Top-Rated SCPs This Month, and Other Top Rated Pages This Month? Shouldn't SCPs on the latter be on the former?

item.title: Mod Post - INFO - Open
item.content:encoded:
This has been corrected.

As a side note, please don't edit pages you shouldn't be editing. Staff maintain pages like this, and shouldn't be altered by non-staff.

Feel free to bring things like this to our attention if you discover them in the future, however, please let staff handle it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: But we now have another problem. The Top Rated Pages per Month 2018 page wasn’t updated with March’s articles.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Zounds! I should have known darker desires were at play behind the sudden request!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Huzzah! Thanks Zyn. Now to see if I can get on it! shill

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I put it back together with scotch tape and dandelion petals, give me some credit for that at least piefishy

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You broke my heart Zyn, YOU BROKE MY HEART

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Updoot.

Let me know if I broke anything, usually I don't change this page.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Soooo is this gonna get updated? I don't mean to be fussy but it's the 4th now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Suggestion; unlock the page so that people can update the page.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Major problem: even though it has moved on to November, and that month’s articles are showing up, the page still says October 2017!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Disregard

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nah. I proposed that, but it wasn't implemented.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh. I could've sworn the page was updated to have the article display the most popular articles of the last 30 days. Probably misremembering, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They were posted in September. This page only displays pages posted in August.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was referring to the two new ones just recently posted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There weren't any GOI format pages posted in August.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Where are the GOI articles? The articles themselves should show up, considering they're tagged.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What, exactly, did you have in mind, if not this?

Having an automated version of what we do now, which is the top-rated articles of the current month (so, as of now, August) and just those top-rated articles posted in the current month.

The point of this endeavor is to make sure that articles will potentially have 30 days on the list no matter when they're posted and to keep it stocked up with recently-posted high-rated pages at all times.

The above quote refers to "top rated pages in the last 30 days", not "top rated pages this month". I thought the latter was what you were referring to in your earlier post, but I misread it.

I can get behind adding a "top rated pages in last 30 days" section to this page, but I personally would like to keep the month-specific partitioning so different months can be compared activity/posting-wise.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It would give us July 4 through August 4. As August progresses, pages posted early in July will fall off the list, such that by August 15, the list will contain articles posted between July 15 and August 15; by the end of the month, it should contain the entirety of August.

The whole point is that, at any given time, the page will show articles posted within the last thirty days or so. Because it is very early in August right now, most of those pages will be those from July, but that's temporary.

The point of this endeavor is to make sure that articles will potentially have 30 days on the list no matter when they're posted and to keep it stocked up with recently-posted high-rated pages at all times.

What, exactly, did you have in mind, if not this?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
created_at="last 1 month"

This would give us modules for July though, wouldn't it? We'd need to rename the page and block headers from "this month" to "last month".

Unless there's a way to automatically generate lists for the current month?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Replace the date="2017.08" in the listpages modules with created_at="last 1 month" and it'll work.

"date=" is, incidentally, deprecated functionality, so we probably shouldn't be using it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really think we should switch to a rolling "past one month" system instead of updating it manually every month.

I agree, and I'm pretty sure wikidot should have some sort of code to accommodate that… The archive for this can probably be put on a separate page, and that can be updated once a month as necessary.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh good, I was just thinking to myself that this page is too useful. Glad that's fixed.

In all seriousness, I really think we should switch to a rolling "past one month" system instead of updating it manually every month. There's barely anything here right now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The articles are still displaying the most popular articles of July. Anyone on staff available to fix that?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not really sure why though.

I mean, I was always under the impression that this was for the 'top rated pages for the month', not the 'majority of pages for the month'. Doing it this way gets messy because you are just displaying all articles above a +10, which is alot. I mean, if you really want that, that's fine… I just thought this was supposed to display the cream of the crop only is all; and the top 20 articles of that month should be more than sufficient to achieve that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
find a way to make it display only the last 30 days and not just each calendar month.

[[module ListPages rating=">10" tags="scp" order="rating desc" separate="false" created_at="last 1 month"]]
%%title_linked%% (+%%rating%%)
[[/module]]

Here's what it would look like today, with the rating minimum bumped up to +20 just to keep the formatting from getting weird. You'll have to imagine for yourself how this matches up against what the current iteration looks like towards the beginning of the month.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Late, but the removal of the "limit="20"" bit has been done to see how it works out for users. We'll wait to see how this change goes over before trying to raise both minimum rating and/or find a way to make it display only the last 30 days and not just each calendar month.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I wouldn't object to removing the limit="20" part from each listpages, just to minimize the chance of something being buried by higher-rated articles when it might be visible in a different month, since that doesn't really reflect on the article in question at all.

If that happens, I'd want the minimum rating to be juiced up a bit, since +10 doesn't scream "quality". +30 as a cutoff seems good.

A more pressing concern is, why is this tied to calendar months and not just a rolling "last thirty days"? It would be a more elegant solution than manually changing it every month, makes sure that every sufficiently highly rated article gets appropriate exposure, and spares the page from ever needing to be empty.

item.title: Re: Updating for May
item.content:encoded: Feex'd.

item.title: Updating for May
item.content:encoded: I see that the SCP box is updated for May, but what about the other three boxes? They're still in April mode. May someone with access amend that bit?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fourthing that I feel all positively rated pages should get a chance to be on the board. If there was also a way to put Skips and Tales side by side, that would be awesome, because I feel scrolling through everything is a bit cumbersome, and Tales are still a criminally underlooked portion of writing overall.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Joining the sentiment that the current arrangement is not optimal. It limits exposure for anything that is not top rated for the month. I mean, those that are top rated will get more votes as they already would draw attention. But what of anything less?

At least the old system allows presence of articles that have less stellar ratings. Of course, this only affects SCPs (for more people write them). But in months where Tales happen to be written en masse, even Tales will fall under too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ditto.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: We manually change the month at the start of each month, but the actual rankings autoupdate as it's a module that displays stuff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really, really, really, really dislike how this page is formatted now. Conceptually, I like the split between SCPs, tales, and other misc pages, but I also liked seeing a very clear indication of the rankings of the articles for each month even when they were mixed. This just seems like an overload of information at once. I am not a fan.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How often does it auto-update? I know there are more new articles this month with more than 13 points.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Linking this directly from the sidebar over the old top rated is the best decision ever.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, the month is far from over, can we make a consistent amount of good articles within the next 27 days with enough time to get upvotes and bury the remanants of our crack party?

20+ after a couple of days is a respectable rating, very few months have more than the 25 articles with ratings that high needed to push them off the front page; so these are likely to stay (unless their authors delete them or something).

If not we will just unpersonmonth April of 2016. What? That month? I don't know what you are talking about. 2016 didn't have an April.

I'd rather not, I like the article I've already posted this month (and may post one to a few more).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, the month is far from over, can we make a consistent amount of good articles within the next 27 days with enough time to get upvotes and bury the remanants of our crack party?

If not we will just unpersonmonth April of 2016. What? That month? I don't know what you are talking about. 2016 didn't have an April.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, even the twothree non-contest titles here, at the time of my posting this, are somewhat weird ones.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It just became to realisation that April 2016 will forever be remembered as the month full of weird-ass titles as top rated pages. Best we don't direct newcomers to that month as the contemporary standard of writing here…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Praise Decibelles. Mod among mods!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: THY WILL BE DONE

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Uh… should this page be updated to have articles that are posted on Feb 2016?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, that's true. But whoever owns these pages keeps forgetting to update. If it were automatic, there'd be no issue with that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So? It's top rated pages this month. You can see the records of top-rated pages every month in the archive for each year at the bottom of the page. Plus it's literally a few seconds of work to update.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think he means that the month of the year has to be changed monthly on the module.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You look at the page source:

 [[module ListPages rating=">5" order="rating desc" separate="false" limit="200" perPage="25" date="2015.02"]] %%title_linked%% (rating: %%rating%%, comments: %%comments%%) [[/module]]

What this does is pull pages from the wiki during the month of February with a rating of over 5. What this does is keep track of the rating and sorts it in order. You can see where I use something vaguely similar here.

For example: here is what it looks like when I upvoted it.
And here is what it looks like when I downvote it.

That's the beautiful thing about modules; this is done all automatically.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No they don't. Look at the page source.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They… they do automatically update. What are you trying to say?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There has to be a way to make this automatically update. As it is right now, eugh.
